response.justification: While the candidate is a strong software engineer with 15+ years of diverse experience, the role has critical disqualifiers. The job explicitly requires native-level Japanese language proficiency—entirely absent from the resume. More fundamentally, this is a specialized embedded networking security role requiring deep domain expertise in network protocols, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel source modification, and switch hardware—none of which appear in the candidate's background. Their recent work centers on web/backend systems (TypeScript/Node.js), with the last C embedded work in 2014, making the technical stack misalignment severe for a mid-senior embedded systems position.
strengths: Extensive software development foundation (15+ years) demonstrating ability to master diverse technology stacks and domains
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD pipelines, DevOps infrastructure, and code quality discipline
Some embedded systems exposure through robotics, hardware orchestration tools, and cross-domain technical work shows systems-level thinking
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency—job requires native-level Japanese, making this a critical blocker for the role
No demonstrated expertise in embedded networking security, network protocols (Layer-2/3), or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption protocols)
Minimal recent C programming experience (last C work in 2014); current stack (TypeScript/Node.js backend) is misaligned with embedded firmware development requirements

key_factors.factor: General engineering competence and learning trajectory
